title: manulea altissima subsp. glabricaulis
intro: Manulea altissima subsp. glabricaulis, also known as the Glabrous-stemmed Manulea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infections. The health benefits of Manulea altissima subsp. glabricaulis include its ability to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Manulea altissima subsp. glabricaulis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
